crabbing_discipline.summary: Literal window-of-2 latch crabbing (root-to-leaf, at most parent+child held at once) plus Lehman & Yao Blink-tree move-right recovery to make that budget safe.
crabbing_discipline.descent: engine/btree/insert.go: Tree.crabInsert (lock root; loop: read node, peek-and-move-right if a right sibling's true lower bound is <= target key, else compute child index, lock child, unlock parent, descend)
crabbing_discipline.split_propagation: engine/btree/insert.go: Tree.propagate + Tree.findParent (independent window-of-2 root-to-childID walk re-derived fresh at each level, never a precomputed ancestor chain)
crabbing_discipline.move_right_fields: engine/btree/node.go: InternalNode.NextSibling (mirrors LeafNode.NextLeaf) and InternalNode.LowKey (fixed-forever true subtree lower bound, distinct from Keys[0]) -- see their doc comments for the full reasoning and the concrete bug they fix

root_split_protection.mechanism: Tree.rootMu sync.Mutex + Tree.root uint64 (tree-level state, separate from per-node latches)
root_split_protection.location: engine/btree/insert.go: Tree struct, Tree.Insert (empty-tree bootstrap under rootMu), Tree.propagate (root-replacement check-and-commit under rootMu)

bug_found_and_fixed_during_implementatio.description: Initial implementation (window-of-2 descent using each node's own currently-populated max key to decide move-right) reproducibly lost keys under concurrent shared-parent stress: a writer could land on a stale ancestor's child whose upper range had already been split off, and (because a sparsely-filled node's own max key can be far below its true range boundary under out-of-order concurrent inserts) never realize it needed to move further right, silently stranding the key in a node not reachable via top-down routing.
bug_found_and_fixed_during_implementatio.fix: Peek the candidate right sibling's true lower bound (leaf: its first key; internal: new InternalNode.LowKey field) instead of comparing against the current node's own max key, in both Tree.crabInsert and Tree.findParent.
bug_found_and_fixed_during_implementatio.verification_of_fix: Ad-hoc repro tests (not part of the committed diff) confirmed the bug pre-fix and its absence post-fix across dozens of runs; TestCrabbingInsert -count=10 and full package -race -count=3 all green (self-consistency only, not formal verification).

new_public_api: type Tree struct{ Store, Alloc, unexported rootMu/root }
func (t *Tree) Insert(path string, fileID uint64) error
backward_compat: Free function Insert(store, alloc, rootNodeID, path, fileID) is unchanged and still used by all pre-existing single-threaded tests; Tree is purely additive.
notes_for_2a.4.3_delete: Delete will need the SAME root-pointer protection approach (Tree.rootMu) if it is added as a Tree method rather than a free function, to stay consistent with insert's root-split handling.

Delete under concurrent crabbing will likely need the identical NextSibling/LowKey move-right recovery at every level it descends through, for the same reason insert did (stale-ancestor misrouting is not insert-specific).
Consider having delete reuse Tree.findParent rather than duplicating an equivalent helper -- it is already generic over childID/path and does not assume anything insert-specific.
engine/btree/insert.go's Tree.propagate's retry-on-indexOfChild-miss loop (concurrent shared-parent-split race) is a pattern delete's merge/rebalance propagation will likely need to mirror.
